chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 7580 of 2022 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For hearing of main case. 17.01.2025 Mr. Khalid Mehmood Khan Kayani, advocate for the petitioners. Malik Altaf Hussain, advocate for the respondent. Mr. Muhammad Kamran Khan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh. This petition seeks retirement benefits on behalf of the deceased, however, as pointed out by the learned counsel for the respondent from page 25 that vide order dated 27.10.2008 the deceased had been given voluntarily retirement and he remained alive till 2012 during which time no claim for same was preferred. Be that as it may, factually this petition is in respect of deceased quota as highlighted in bold by petitioner in the prayer clause. The Supreme Court has already declared the same to be unlawful vide judgment dated 26.09.2024 in the case of General Post Office Islamabad vs. Muhammad Jalal (Civil Petition No.3390 of 2021). It has also been reported that Rule 11-A of the Sindh Civil Servants (Appointment, Promotion & Transfer) Rules, 1974, has already been deleted by the Sindh Cabinet on 04.12.2024. In view of the foregoing this petition is prima facie misconceived, hence, the same is dismissed. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
